No task label given. Min-max scaling case 1: values=[10, 12, 13, 15, 20]. Scale each value to [0,1].

Task classification: minmax_scaling. Known inputs: values=[10, 12, 13, 15, 20]. Missing or invalid inputs: None. Calculator plan: {"calculator": "minmax_scaling", "inputs": {"values": [10, 12, 13, 15, 20]}, "expected_outputs": ["min", "max", "scaled"], "requires_exact_calculator": true} Interpretation: The minmax_scaling calculator was applied to the input values [10, 12, 13, 15, 20]. The result is scaled_values=[0.0, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5, 1.0]. This scaling maps the original range of 10 to 20 to the range of 0 to 1, as expected.

For one logistic regression example x=[-2, 2], w=[-0.4, -0.3], y=0, lambda=0.05, compute prediction and L2-regularized gradient.

Task classification: logistic_l2_gradient_numeric. Known inputs: x=[-2, 2], w=[-0.4, -0.3], y=0, lambda=0.05. Missing or invalid inputs: None. Calculator plan: {"calculator": "logistic_l2_gradient_numeric", "inputs": {"x": [-2, 2], "w": [-0.4, -0.3], "y": 0, "lambda": 0.05}, "expected_outputs": ["p", "gradient"], "requires_exact_calculator": true} Interpretation: Problem: Compute logistic regression gradient with L2 regularization. Method: For one example, p=sigmoid(w^T x) and grad_w=(p-y)x + lambda*w. Calculation: z=-0.4*-2+-0.3*2=0.2. p=sigmoid(z)=0.5498. grad=[(0.5498-0)*-2+0.05*-0.4, (0.5498-0)*2+0.05*-0.3] = [-1.1197, 1.0847]. Result: p=0.5498 and gradient=[-1.1197, 1.0847].

Method: Relate the observed loss/gradient pattern to the gradient descent update rule. Calculation: Clip gradient norm by g <- g * threshold / ||g|| when ||g|| > threshold.
